note:
苹果联合创始人史蒂夫·沃兹尼亚克(Steve Wozniak)起诉视频分享巨头YouTube及其母公司谷歌(Google)涉嫌允许利用他的肖像在其平台上蓬勃发展的比特币骗局。
沃兹尼亚克是周二提起诉讼的18名原告之一，诉讼寻求惩罚性赔偿，由陪审团进行审判，并要求YouTube删除所有使用沃兹尼亚克的名字和肖像的比特币赠品骗局和促销活动。
诉讼赞扬Twitter“迅速而果断地”关闭恶意账户，并“保护其用户免受诈骗”，指的是该平台对上周的协同网络攻击的回应，那次攻击获得了一系列经过验证的Twitter账户，并发布了一条加密泄密信息。
诉讼称：“与此形成鲜明对比的是，几个月来，被告YouTube一直毫无歉意地主持、推广并直接从类似的诈骗中获利。”
沃兹尼亚克并不是第一个就密码诈骗对YouTube采取行动的人。今年早些时候，Ripple Labs与首席执行官布拉德·加林豪斯(Brad Garlinghouse)一起起诉该平台，称其未能有效监管虚假的XRP赠品骗局，这些骗局给公司造成了金钱和声誉损害。
根据提交给圣马特奥县加利福尼亚州高等法院的新起诉书，YouTube“提供了源源不断的诈骗视频和促销活动，虚假使用原告史蒂夫·沃兹尼亚克(Steve Wozniak)和其他著名科技企业家的图片和视频，骗取了YouTube用户数百万美元。”
诉讼称，包括比尔·盖茨(Bill Gates)、埃隆·马斯克(Elon Musk)和迈克尔·戴尔(Michael Dell)在内的其他知名企业家的形象和肖像也在这些骗局中被利用。
根据起诉书中附上的截图，涉及沃兹尼亚克的骗局使用的图片和视频告诉用户，这位企业家正在主持现场比特币或“比特币”赠品活动。诉讼称，这些帖子“说服”用户转移他们的加密货币，承诺在有限的时间内，他们“将收到两倍的回报”。
诉状称：“YouTube和谷歌更进一步，通过提供针对最有可能受到伤害的用户的付费广告，推广这些骗局并从中获利。”
沃兹尼亚克指控被告YouTube和谷歌侵犯他的公开权，盗用他的名字和肖像，以及协助和教唆欺诈，以及疏忽未能警告用户。
起诉书称：“被告未能发出警告是故意的、恶意的、压迫的、欺诈性的和/或不顾原告权利的，从而使原告有权获得惩罚性赔偿。”
这起诉讼要求陪审团对所有可审判的问题进行审判，并对包括法律费用在内的损害赔偿进行审判，以及任何“被告不当获得的收益、利润或利益”。

note_en:
Apple co-founder Steve Wozniak is suing video-sharing giant YouTube and its parent company Google for allegedly allowing bitcoin giveaway scams that use his likeness to thrive on its platform.
 Wozniak was one of 18 plaintiffs that filed the lawsuit on Tuesday, which seeks punitive damages, a trial by jury and demands YouTube remove all bitcoin giveaway scams and promotions using Wozniak’s name and likeness.
 The suit praised Twitter for acting “swiftly and decisively” to shut down malicious accounts and “protect its users from the scam” referencing the platform’s response to last week’s  coordinated cyberattack that gained access to a host of verified Twitter accounts and posted a crypto giveaway message.
 “In stark contrast, for months now, Defendant YOUTUBE has been unapologetically hosting, promoting, and directly profiting from similar scams,” the suit said.
 Wozniak is not the first to take action against YouTube over crypto scams. Earlier this year, Ripple Labs, along with CEO Brad Garlinghouse,  sued the platform for allegedly failing to effectively police fake XRP giveaway scams that were causing monetary and reputational harm to the company.
 According to the new complaint filed with the Superior Court of the State of California in the county of San Mateo, YouTube has “featured a steady stream of scam videos and promotions that falsely use images and videos of Plaintiff Steve Wozniak, and other famous tech entrepreneurs, and that have defrauded YouTube users out of millions of dollars.”
 The suit alleged that the image and likeness of other well-known entrepreneurs including Bill Gates, Elon Musk and Michael Dell were also being exploited in these scams.
 According to screenshots attached in the complaint, the scams involving Wozniak uses images and videos that tell users that the entrepreneur is hosting a live bitcoin or “BTC” giveaway event. The suit alleges that the posts “convince” users to transfer their cryptocurrency promising that, for a limited time, they “will receive twice as much back”.
 “YOUTUBE and GOOGLE took the further step of promoting and profiting from these scams by providing paid advertising that targeted users who were most likely to be harmed,” the suit said.
 Wozniak is accusing defendants YouTube and Google of violating his right of publicity, misappropriating his name and likeness, as well as aiding and abetting fraud, and negligent failure to warn users.
 “Defendants’ failure to warn was willful, malicious, oppressive, fraudulent, and/or in reckless disregard of the Plaintiffs’ rights, thereby entitling Plaintiffs to punitive damages,” the suit said.
 The suit demands a trial by jury on all issues triable, and damages that include legal expenses, and any “gains, profits, or advantages wrongfully obtained by Defendants.”

title_en: Apple Co-Founder Steve Wozniak Sues YouTube over Bitcoin Giveaway Scams

根据研究，德里2800万居民中有近四分之一可能感染了冠状病毒抗体，使其成为世界上受影响最严重的首府城市之一。
印度国家疾病控制中心(NCDC)对2万名居民进行了随机抽样，发现23.4%的居民有该病毒抗体。似乎大多数人都没有症状。
上个月，冠状病毒危机重创德里，将这座城市的医疗系统推向崩溃的边缘，许多人在医院用完床位和呼吸机后死亡。然而，NCDC研究中发现的暴露速度远远高于该市官方报告的123,747例，这表明冠状病毒在印度的传播可能远远超过官方统计数据显示的程度。
印度的病例数量位居世界第三，为119万例，每天增加超过4万例。
感染曲线没有显示出平坦的迹象，印度各地，从喀拉拉邦到克什米尔，都重新实施了个人封锁和夜间宵禁，以努力控制病毒的传播。
根据印度为数不多的几家获得授权进行抗体测试的私人实验室之一本周发布的一项研究结果，估计有1.8亿印度人，占总人口的近15%，可能已经产生了新冠肺炎抗体。
Thyrocare检查了印度各地600个地点在20天内进行的6万次测试的数据，发现在孟买测试的人中有27.3%-相当于500万人-有抗体。最高的数字，47%，发生在同样位于马哈拉施特拉邦的比万迪(Bhiwandi)。
这些数据不是像德里那样的随机抽样，而是基于积极对抗体测试进行排序的人，这可能会扭曲结果。
但这两项研究都对印度新冠肺炎异常低的死亡人数提出了进一步的质疑，印度的死亡人数为28,732人，低于英国、法国和意大利等较小国家的死亡人数，这些国家的报告病例要少得多。
印度在收集死亡数据方面的记录很差，只有大约77%的死亡登记在案，只有22%的人有医学证明的死因。
正如印度领先的死亡率专家Prabhat Jha博士指出的那样，该国大约80%的死亡发生在农村地区，在那里他们经常没有上报。他说，这种死亡数据的缺乏可能会严重歪曲新冠肺炎的官方死亡人数。
“总体而言，我们根本不知道印度发生了什么，因为在马哈拉施特拉邦以外没有系统的死亡数据，”他说。“在我们做到这一点之前，我们不会了解印度到底发生了什么，这个国家将很难走出大流行。”
中央邦、西孟加拉邦和古吉拉特邦等邦的火葬场和墓地工作人员的多份报告显示，死亡人数远远高于记录显示的数字。根据“英国医学杂志”的一篇报道，在古吉拉特邦的瓦多达拉市，新冠肺炎阳性病例中近75%的死亡被归因于其他原因，如糖尿病或器官移植等并发症。
Jha说，印度的年轻人口-全国只有5%的人超过65岁-可能是导致低死亡率的一个因素。然而，他补充说，印度冠状病毒死亡率的特别缓慢的轨迹仍然“令人费解”。
在马哈拉施特拉邦等受灾严重的州，死亡人数花了一个月的时间才翻了一番，达到目前的1.2万人，相比之下，纽约和意大利北部的死亡人数最高时每7天就会翻一番。
当印度本周开始通过与美国和法国之间的所谓“气泡”开放国际边境时，全国各地都在重新对跨越国界的人实施封锁和严格的政策。
果阿本周将开始对每一个进入该邦的人进行快速测试，而周三，克什米尔全境实施了为期6天的封锁，那里的医疗系统正努力应对病例飙升以及呼吸机和氧气罐严重短缺的问题。

Almost a quarter of Delhi’s 28 million residents may have developed coronavirus antibodies, making it one of the worst-affected capital cities in the world, according to research.
 A random sample of 20,000 residents by India’s National Centre for Disease Control (NCDC) found 23.4% had antibodies to the virus. It appears that the majority were asymptomatic.
 The coronavirus crisis  hit Delhi hard last month, pushing the city’s healthcare system to the brink of collapse, and many died after hospitals ran out of beds and ventilators. Yet the rate of exposure found in the NCDC study is far higher than the city’s officially reported 123,747 cases, suggesting the spread of coronavirus in India may be far greater than the official statistics show.
 India has the third highest number of cases in the world, 1.19m, which is rising by more than 40,000 a day.
  The curve of infections is showing no sign of flattening and states across India, from Kerala to Kashmir, have reintroduced individual lockdowns and night-time curfews in an effort to control the spread of the virus.
 According to results of a study released this week by one of the few private laboratories in India authorised to conduct antibody tests, an estimated 180 million Indians, nearly 15% of the population, may already have developed Covid-19 antibodies.
  Thyrocare examined data from 60,000 tests conducted in 600 locations across India over 20 days and found that 27.3% of those tested in Mumbai – equating to 5 million people – had the antibodies. The highest figure, 47%, was in Bhiwandi, a city also in Maharashtra state.
 Rather than a random sample like in Delhi, the data was based on people who actively sort an antibodies test, which is likely to have skewed the results.
 But both studies have raised further questions over India’s unusually low Covid-19 death toll, which at 28,732 is less than those of smaller countries such as the UK, France and Italy, which have had far fewer reported cases.
  India has a poor record on collecting mortality data, with only about 77% of deaths registered and only 22% having a medically certified cause of death.
 As Dr Prabhat Jha, a leading expert on mortality in India, pointed out, about 80% of the country’s deaths occur in rural areas where they often go unreported. He said this absence of mortality data was likely to heavily skew the official number of deaths from Covid-19.
 “Overall, we simply don’t know what’s happing in India because outside of Maharashtra there is no systematic mortality data,” he said. “Until we have that, we will not understand what is really going on in India and the country will struggle to move out of the pandemic.”
 Multiple reports from crematorium and graveyard workers in states such as Madhya Pradesh, West Bengal and Gujarat suggest a far higher number of the dead than records show. According to a report in the British Medical Journal, in the Gujarat city of Vadodara, nearly 75% of deaths in Covid-19 positive cases were being attributed to others causes such complications from diabetes or organ transplants.
 Jha said India’s young population – only 5% of the country is over 65 – was likely to be a factor in the low mortality rate. However, he added that the particularly slow trajectory of the rate of coronavirus deaths in India still remained “puzzling”.
 In badly hit states such as Maharashtra it has taken a month for the toll to double to the current figure of 12,000, compared with New York and northern Italy where at the peak the total number of dead was doubling every seven days.
 While India began opening its international borders this week through so-called “air bubbles” with the US and France, states across the country were reintroducing lockdowns and strict policies for people crossing state borders.
 Goa will this week begin rapidly testing everyone who enters the state, while on Wednesday a six-day lockdown was imposed across Kashmir, where the healthcare system is struggling to cope with a soaring increase in cases and an acute shortage of ventilators and oxygen tanks.
title: 研究发现，德里近四分之一的地区可能感染了冠状病毒
title_en: Almost a quarter of Delhi may have had coronavirus, finds study
